visual_attributes.morphology: Medium-sized antelope with a distinctive large, downward-facing proboscis.
distinctive_features: Thick, slightly translucent wax-colored horns with pronounced rings
visual_attributes.texture: Smooth in summer, thicker and woolly in winter
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: Head-and-body length 100-140 cm, shoulder height 61-81 cm, weight 26-69 kg
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: Compact body with short tail and long legs

domain_specific_visual.HORN_LENGTH: 28-38 cm for Russian saiga, up to 22 cm for Mongolian saiga
domain_specific_visual.MANE_LENGTH: 12 to 15 cm long mane on the neck in winter

domain_specific_functional.MIGRATION_PATTERN: Extensive migrations to escape natural calamities
domain_specific_functional.PREDATOR_AVOIDANCE: Vulnerable to wolves, juveniles targeted by foxes and eagles

relationships.description: Phylogenetically related as sister species within Antilopinae
Current populations found in Kazakhstan, Russia, and Mongolia

contextual_attributes.geographic_range: Kalmykia, Astrakhan Oblast, Ural Mountains, Ustyurt Plateau, Betpak-Dala, western Mongolia
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: Characteristic animal of Scythia, important to Andronovo culture
